chief_complaint: A 65-year-old male patient presented with persistent chest pain.
history: The patient initially presented with acute anterior myocardial infarction symptoms, including electrocardiogram findings of coved ST-segment elevation and significantly elevated myocardial enzymes. He was admitted for persistent chest pain and underwent percutaneous coronary intervention.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 100 bpm, RR 24/min. Physical examination revealed a patient in distress with a blood pressure drop.
labs: ECG: coved ST-segment elevation. Myocardial enzymes: significantly elevated. Bedside ultrasound: ascending aortic dissection.
correct_diagnosis: Acute type A aortic dissection (TA-AAD) with acute myocardial infarction (AMI)
differentials: Acute type A aortic dissection (TA-AAD) with acute myocardial infarction (AMI)
correct_ranking: Acute type A aortic dissection (TA-AAD) with acute myocardial infarction (AMI)
